summary: Added a first-class blocked-slice unblocker policy to the automation harness. Blocked/deferred slices can now name a recommended_unblocker, supervisor dry-run can report parked work with --include-blocked, and the queue now includes the first concrete unblocker slice for clean TestFlight build preparation instead of making blocked TestFlight proof executable prematurely.

contract_status_changes.before: Blocked slices had entry conditions, but the supervisor did not expose a blocked-work report or a machine-readable unblocker relationship.
contract_status_changes.after: Blocked/deferred slices can name `recommended_unblocker`, `run_next.py --dry-run --include-blocked` reports missing entry conditions and unblockers, and queue integrity rejects unknown unblocker IDs.

contract_status_changes.before: TestFlight proof slices were correctly blocked, but there was no queued preparatory slice that could make progress without claiming TestFlight proof.
contract_status_changes.after: `owlory-release-clean-testflight-build-prep` is queued as the smallest immediate unblocker; TestFlight proof retry/capture remain blocked until their entry conditions are actually true.

residual_risks: The harness now reports recommended unblockers, but it does not auto-create unblocker slices. That remains deliberate to avoid inventing work without scoped queue records.

`app-localization-review-packet-for-first-locale` and `owlory-ui-regression-next-surface-triage` are represented as deferred unblocker records. They should not be made executable until their own entry conditions are true.
recommended_next_reason: This is the highest-priority queued unblocker and prepares the release/TestFlight gate without claiming TestFlight proof.
